channel.title: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Why Macky Sall Can’t Count on Africa in His Bid to Lead the UN
item.description: Macky Sall, a former Senegalese president, is continuing to meet with countries bilaterally to shore up support for his candidacy for the top position at the United Nations even as an endorsement from the African Union, or AU, remains out of reach. Burundi, which chairs the AU, backs Sall officially, but that has not led ... Read more

item.title: Guardians of the Sea: How GEF Small Grants Program Enables Young Volunteers Take the Lead in Sea Turtle Conservation
item.description: Every winter thousands of sea turtles come ashore at Cox’s Bazar, in the Bay of Bengal, Bangladesh, to lay eggs. Their path to their breeding grounds is hazardous – fishing nets, propellers, light pollution, coastal developments, stray dogs and other dangers conspire against their success. The area is rich in biodiversity, with five out of […]

item.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 20 April: Uncertainty grows in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Tensions have escalated in the Strait of Hormuz amid renewed security incidents, including reported attacks on vessels and a US interception of an Iranian cargo ship. In Lebanon, a 10-day ceasefire with Israel continues to hold, with thousands returning to the south despite widespread destruction. A UN peacekeeper was also killed over the weekend – likely from Hezbollah fire.  Stay with us for live updates. App users can follow coverage here.

item.title: Time running out on development goals as finance dries up, UN warns
item.description: Rising conflicts, the climate crisis and shrinking development finance are putting growing pressure on the poorest and most vulnerable countries – pushing development goals further off track.

item.title: Gaza: Human development set back 77 years as recovery costs rise to $71 billion
item.description: The UN and European Union issued a joint warning on Monday that human development across Gaza has been set back by a staggering 77 years, with $71.4 billion needed over the next decade for recovery and reconstruction.

item.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Emergency briefing on Ukraine amid escalating Russian attacks
item.description: The Security Council meets in emergency session at 3pm to address the escalating conflict in Ukraine. The open briefing follows a formal request from Kyiv citing a surge in Russian aerial bombardments, including devastating strikes on the city of Dnipro. Ukraine has reported that between late March and mid-April, more than 5,000 drones and missiles were launched, killing dozens of civilians and injuring hundreds more. Follow full meetings coverage below and UN News app users can follow here.

item.title: UN Forum puts spotlight on healthcare for Indigenous Peoples
item.description: Ensuring Indigenous Peoples’ access to healthcare, including during conflict, is the theme for a major meeting that opened at United Nations Headquarters in New York on Monday.

item.title: Russia’s African Cannon Fodder
item.description: On 7 April, the government of Cameroon published a list of 16 of its citizens confirmed killed fighting for Russia against Ukraine. That means the number of Cameroon citizens killed in this distant war has likely surpassed a hundred, making the country the biggest victim of a Russian recruitment drive increasingly focused on Africa. Conflict […]

item.title: Using Better Data to Break the Cycle of Permanent Crisis
item.description: We are stuck in response mode. But what good is an ambulance without a hospital? Climate shocks are intensifying. Conflict is at record levels. Economies are fragile. Humanitarian appeals grow larger each year, while donor countries prioritise domestic and security concerns. One emergency follows another. Recovery slips further out of reach. For years, the logic […]

item.title: Aid Groups Appeal for Lasting Ceasefire to Address Lebanon’s Catastrophic Humanitarian Crisis
item.description: Aid groups have welcomed a ten-day ceasefire agreed between Israel and Lebanon but warn only a permanent halt to fighting can allow for the kind of response needed to address the dire humanitarian situation in the country. A ten-day truce ​to enable peace negotiations between the two countries came into effect on April 16. It […]
